response.justification: Bogdan has a long career with strong software craftsmanship values and genuine IDE exposure at National Instruments (LabWindows/CVI), but his primary expertise for over a decade is firmly in TypeScript, Node.js, and DLang — not the Java/Kotlin/JVM stack this role demands. Java appears in his skills list and was used peripherally at Altom for robotic arms and a device orchestration tool, but there is no evidence of deep JVM understanding, Spring or enterprise framework experience, or any engagement with JVM-based IDE internals. The role requires exactly the depth in Java/Kotlin enterprise tooling that this resume does not demonstrate.
strengths: Prior contribution to an IDE product (NI LabWindows/CVI) shows real exposure to the developer-tooling domain
Consistent software craftsmanship culture across roles — TDD, pairing, CI/CD, and trunk-based development practiced deliberately
Demonstrated ability to own complex, high-scale systems (Sociomantic: millions of HTTP requests/second; GISCollective: distributed geo-data platform)
concerns: No meaningful Java or Kotlin expertise demonstrated — Java is listed in skills but only used peripherally for robotic arms and a mobile device orchestration tool
Zero evidence of Spring, Quarkus, Hibernate, or any JVM enterprise framework experience, which are central to the role
Primary stack for the bulk of his career is TypeScript/Node.js/DLang with no visible investment in or interest toward the JVM ecosystem

key_factors.factor: No Spring or JVM enterprise framework experience
Prior IDE tooling work at National Instruments
